title: Slow-cooker Moroccan chicken with olives and preserved lemon
subtitle: Chicken thighs with cumin, green olives, and preserved lemon
excerpt: Bone-in chicken thighs braised with green olives, preserved lemon, cumin, coriander, and turmeric for five to six hours. The preserved lemon and olives lift the sauce into something more interesting than a plain chicken braise. Serve with couscous or flatbread.
sourceNotes: Chicken with olives and preserved lemon is a Moroccan dish called djej mqualli, one of the central recipes of Moroccan home cooking. The slow cooker version became common in British home kitchens after preserved lemons became widely available in supermarkets. The dish is finished with the olive and preserved lemon combination, which provides salt, brine, and a floral citrus note in the final minutes.

recipe.makeAheadNotes: Better the next day. The preserved lemon and olives become part of the sauce as it rests.

content.text: Add the preserved lemon rind in the last 30 minutes rather than from the start. Preserved lemon added too early loses its brightness and becomes bitter. The olives can go in from the beginning — they hold up to the long cook without problem.

items.prepNote: taste first — preserved lemon is salty

content.text: Add the {{chicken-thigh}}, {{olives-green}}, {{onion}}, {{garlic}}, {{ginger-root}}, {{cumin-ground}}, {{coriander-ground}}, {{turmeric}}, {{sea-salt-fine}}, and {{black-pepper}} to the slow cooker. Pour in the {{stock-chicken}}. Cook on low for 4.5 to 5.5 hours. Add the {{preserved-lemon}} rind in the last 30 minutes. Taste before adding further salt — the preserved lemon and olives contribute salt throughout the cook. Serve with couscous, yoghurt, and fresh herbs.

items.cause: Preserved lemon added too early and dissolved into the sauce, or olives are very salty
items.fix: Add preserved lemon in the last 30 minutes only. Rinse brine-packed olives before adding. Taste before seasoning — this dish rarely needs added salt

items.symptom: Preserved lemon flavour not coming through
items.cause: Added from the beginning and lost its brightness
items.fix: Stir in a little fresh preserved lemon rind after cooking. Even a small amount added at the end provides a clear citrus note
Reduce the stock to 150 ml. The chicken releases moisture during the cook so less stock than expected is needed

content.text: Moroccan chicken with olives and preserved lemon is one of the most elegant slow-cooker chicken dishes in terms of flavour complexity for effort. The three key flavours (the spice base, the olives, and the preserved lemon) do the work. Serve with couscous mixed with toasted flaked almonds and dried apricots for a full Moroccan table.
